---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: e6833a1ebbc2d22fb42e3fcd52640a66de12d1ca
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-1, kind: judged, text: "story dc-2 lets a truth-side shortName collision demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage even when every line of the flowchart is inside the declared grammar. Parent dc-3 defines the tiers by vocabulary — full is 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial is 'beyond it' — so dc-2 introduces a second, undeclared cause of partial that contradicts that definition. Worse, dc-2 admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme': a pristine generator-emitted diagram whose node ids carry flowmap's own disambiguation for colliding names may be unmatchable by shortName(fqn), making full coverage unreachable for a generator-produced artifact — exactly the class of gap dc-1 says a judged sweep already caught for %% comments and classDefs, corrected there but reintroduced here with only prose disclosure and no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3 (confidence 0.62)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-dcj-2, kind: judged, text: "parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. Story dc-3 regenerates with --entry instead, on the theory that --root is render-time-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space — but dc-3 itself concedes this equivalence is only build-time-verifiable, not a closed fact ('a real flowmap accepting a --root value --entry rejects would be a build-time invention-ledger finding'). Until proven, verification may regenerate at a DIFFERENT scope than the pinned field declares, contradicting dc-6's same-scope requirement; the substitution is disclosed in prose but carries no declared supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-dcj-3, kind: judged, text: "story dc-1 places subgraph blocks OUTSIDE the claimed grammar on the assertion they are 'a rollup/C3-view construct, not the plain call-graph flowchart this story regenerates against'. That assertion is unevidenced in the decision (unlike the --entry/--root claim in dc-3, which cites verdi-go help text): if any flowmap graph-mode invocation this story verifies against ever emits subgraph wrappers, a pristine generator-produced diagram would fall to partial, contradicting parent dc-3's 'full = within the generator's vocabulary' — the identical failure mode dc-1 acknowledges was already caught once for %% comments and classDefs (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:6a4ed8095bdc149c99a959a988dddde7d957222c73f41585d93a19800e49668a
integrity: sha256:8833e9d0135807ec80755c8ddfebd09a97d087ac50cc245837fbb78ae8f6173c
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dcj-1\",\"text\":\"story dc-2 lets a truth-side shortName collision demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage even when every line of the flowchart is inside the declared grammar. Parent dc-3 defines the tiers by vocabulary — full is 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial is 'beyond it' — so dc-2 introduces a second, undeclared cause of partial that contradicts that definition. Worse, dc-2 admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme': a pristine generator-emitted diagram whose node ids carry flowmap's own disambiguation for colliding names may be unmatchable by shortName(fqn), making full coverage unreachable for a generator-produced artifact — exactly the class of gap dc-1 says a judged sweep already caught for %% comments and classDefs, corrected there but reintroduced here with only prose disclosure and no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3\",\"confidence\":0.62,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-2\",\"text\":\"parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. Story dc-3 regenerates with --entry instead, on the theory that --root is render-time-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space — but dc-3 itself concedes this equivalence is only build-time-verifiable, not a closed fact ('a real flowmap accepting a --root value --entry rejects would be a build-time invention-ledger finding'). Until proven, verification may regenerate at a DIFFERENT scope than the pinned field declares, contradicting dc-6's same-scope requirement; the substitution is disclosed in prose but carries no declared supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-3\",\"text\":\"story dc-1 places subgraph blocks OUTSIDE the claimed grammar on the assertion they are 'a rollup/C3-view construct, not the plain call-graph flowchart this story regenerates against'. That assertion is unevidenced in the decision (unlike the --entry/--root claim in dc-3, which cites verdi-go help text): if any flowmap graph-mode invocation this story verifies against ever emits subgraph wrappers, a pristine generator-produced diagram would fall to partial, contradicting parent dc-3's 'full = within the generator's vocabulary' — the identical failure mode dc-1 acknowledges was already caught once for %% comments and classDefs\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}\n```" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-2 lets a truth-side shortName collision demote the WHOLE artifact to partial coverage even when every line of the flowchart is inside the declared grammar. Parent dc-3 defines the tiers by vocabulary — full is 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial is 'beyond it' — so dc-2 introduces a second, undeclared cause of partial that contradicts that definition. Worse, dc-2 admits it is 'honestly narrower than flowmap's internal collision-disambiguation scheme': a pristine generator-emitted diagram whose node ids carry flowmap's own disambiguation for colliding names may be unmatchable by shortName(fqn), making full coverage unreachable for a generator-produced artifact — exactly the class of gap dc-1 says a judged sweep already caught for %% comments and classDefs, corrected there but reintroduced here with only prose disclosure and no supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-3 (confidence 0.62)
- **judged-dcj-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: parent dc-6 pins the proposal's scope field as 'the flowmap --root selector it was generated under' and requires verification to 'regenerate truth at the same scope'. Story dc-3 regenerates with --entry instead, on the theory that --root is render-time-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space — but dc-3 itself concedes this equivalence is only build-time-verifiable, not a closed fact ('a real flowmap accepting a --root value --entry rejects would be a build-time invention-ledger finding'). Until proven, verification may regenerate at a DIFFERENT scope than the pinned field declares, contradicting dc-6's same-scope requirement; the substitution is disclosed in prose but carries no declared supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-dcj-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-1 places subgraph blocks OUTSIDE the claimed grammar on the assertion they are 'a rollup/C3-view construct, not the plain call-graph flowchart this story regenerates against'. That assertion is unevidenced in the decision (unlike the --entry/--root claim in dc-3, which cites verdi-go help text): if any flowmap graph-mode invocation this story verifies against ever emits subgraph wrappers, a pristine generator-produced diagram would fall to partial, contradicting parent dc-3's 'full = within the generator's vocabulary' — the identical failure mode dc-1 acknowledges was already caught once for %% comments and classDefs (confidence 0.30)
